raw_consumer_statement:
Good day. 

I purchased a vacuum from XXXX on XX/XX/year>. I had not received the vacuum by the date I was informed it would arrive. I contacted XXXX via XXXX and received no help. I reached out to American Express and filed a dispute. 

American Express has focused on XXXX  having a tracking number and with that has ruled I did indeed receive my package. However, that is not the case. I have tried to speak with the Dispute Team at American Express, however I have been denied that right. 

If American Express is going to solely focus on XXXX 's tracking number, then lets look at the tracking number. The tracking number, XXXX says my package was delivered on XX/XX/year>. The tracking number gives the city, but no house address. The picture attached to the listed tracking number is not a vacuum nor is it my house. My house is brick on the bottom not wood. 

I am asking for someone to be my advocate and review this case. I did not get my vacuum and American Express is not investigating this dispute appropriately. 
I have supplied American Express with a lot of proof that I did not indeed receive my vacuum. However, they have solely ruled I did by XXXX having a tracking number. 
I will supply all documentation upon request. 

Regards, XXXX
